Interactive Training Tutorial Santiago Rojas & Garrett Woodhouse Welcome Welcome to the tutorial to Build an Interactive Video Training. Use the menu on the left to navigate through the different steps. If you have any questions please contact me via email: santiago.rojasotero@usu.edu
1 Setup Programs 1.1 Install Unity 1.2 Install Meta Link 1.3 Verify the Installation

1 Setup Programs Before creating an interactive training, install and configure the required software: Unity Hub and Unity Editor Meta Link Complete each section below before continuing to the next chapter. 1.1 Install Unity Unity will be used to create and edit the interactive training project. 1.1.1 Step 1: Create a Unity account Create a free Unity account, or sign in if you already have one. 1.1.2 Step 2: Download Unity Hub Open the link below and download Unity Hub: Download Unity Personal 1.1.3 Step 3: Install Unity Open the Unity Hub installer. Follow the installation instructions. Sign in with your Unity account. Use Unity Hub to install the required version of the Unity Editor. Note: Unity Hub manages your Unity projects and installed Unity Editor versions. 1.2 Install Meta Link Meta Link allows your computer to connect to a compatible Meta Quest headset. 1.2.1 Step 1: Download Meta Link Open the installation page below: Download and install Meta Link 1.2.2 Step 2: Install the program Download the installer. Open the downloaded file. Follow the installation instructions. Sign in with your Meta account when prompted. Important: Do not connect the headset until the installation process asks you to do so. 1.3 Verify the Installation Before continuing, confirm that both programs work correctly. Unity Hub opens without displaying an error. You can sign in to your Unity account. At least one Unity Editor version is installed. The Meta Link application opens correctly. Your Meta Quest headset is recognized by the computer. After completing this checklist, continue to the next chapter.

2 Design the Training Plan 2.1 Step 1: Select the Video Format 2.2 Step 2: Prepare the Questions 2.3 Step 3: Prepare the Instructions 2.4 Step 4: Prepare the Feedback 2.5 Step 5: Identify When Each Element Will Appear 2.6 Step 6: Install Unity and Unity Hub 2.7 Step 7: Save the Project Template

2 Design the Training Plan Before opening Unity, prepare the structure and content of your interactive training. Planning the training in advance will make it easier to configure the project template and reduce errors during development. This chapter will guide you through the following tasks: Selecting the video format Preparing the questions and answer options Writing instructions and feedback Identifying when each element will appear Opening the project template in Unity Completing the planning documents 2.1 Step 1: Select the Video Format Begin by deciding how the training video should respond to the participant’s answers. There are two available formats. 2.1.1 Option 1: Continuous Video With Embedded Questions In this format, the participant watches one continuous video. At predetermined points: The video pauses. A question appears on the screen. The participant selects an answer. The video resumes. The process repeats when the next question is reached. Recommended use: Choose this format when all participants should watch the same video sequence while answering questions that assess their understanding. 2.1.2 Option 2: Branching Video With Different Outcomes In this format, the video sequence changes depending on the participant’s answer. For example: A correct answer may continue to the next part of the training. An incorrect answer may show corrective feedback. A selected answer may lead to a different video, scene, or outcome. Recommended use: Choose this format when participants’ decisions should affect the storyline, setting, or sequence of the training. To use a branching format, prepare all required materials in advance, including: Each video segment The instructions presented during each segment The questions The answer options The correct answer for each question The feedback presented after incorrect answers The destination video or scene associated with each answer 2.1.3 Video Format Comparison Feature Continuous Video Branching Video Uses one primary video sequence Yes Not necessarily Questions pause the video Yes Yes All participants see the same sequence Yes No Answers can change the storyline No Yes Requires multiple prepared video segments No Usually Complexity Lower Higher 2.2 Step 2: Prepare the Questions Determine how many questions will appear in each training module. For every question, prepare: The question text The possible answer options The correct answer The feedback associated with each answer Current limitation: The project currently includes templates for questions with either two or four answer options. Use the following format when planning each question: Component Example Question What should the caregiver do next? Answer A Remove the hearing aid Answer B Redirect the child to the activity Correct answer Answer B Correct-answer feedback Correct. Redirecting helps the child continue participating. Incorrect-answer feedback Try again. Removing the hearing aid may reinforce the behavior. 2.3 Step 3: Prepare the Instructions Write the instructions that participants will see before each question. The instructions should tell participants what to observe or consider while watching the video. For example: Instruction: Watch how the caregiver responds when the child attempts to remove the hearing aid. Keep each instruction: Short Clear Directly related to the upcoming question Visible long enough for the participant to read it 2.4 Step 4: Prepare the Feedback Write the feedback that will appear after the participant selects an answer. Prepare feedback for: The correct answer Each incorrect answer 2.4.1 Correct-Answer Feedback Correct-answer feedback should: Confirm that the selected answer was correct Briefly explain why it was correct Reinforce the important training concept 2.4.2 Incorrect-Answer Feedback Incorrect-answer feedback should: Indicate that the selected answer was incorrect Explain why the answer was not appropriate Redirect the participant toward the relevant instruction Allow the participant to try again, when applicable Tip: Feedback should teach the participant, not only indicate whether the answer was correct or incorrect. 2.5 Step 5: Identify When Each Element Will Appear Determine the exact time at which each instruction and question should appear in the video. For each item, record: The instruction appearance time The question appearance time The video location associated with the item What should happen after the participant answers For example, an instruction may appear: One minute before the related question Immediately before a target behavior occurs Immediately after a specific scenario is shown At the beginning of a new training segment 2.5.1 Example Timeline Video Time Element Content Action 02:00 Instruction Observe how the caregiver responds. Continue playing 03:00 Question 1 What should the caregiver do next? Pause video 05:00 Instruction Pay attention to the child’s communication. Continue playing 06:00 Question 2 Which response is most appropriate? Pause video 2.6 Step 6: Install Unity and Unity Hub Before opening the project template: Download Unity Hub. Create or sign in to your Unity account. Install the required version of the Unity Editor. Confirm that Unity Hub opens correctly. Refer to the Setup Programs chapter for detailed installation instructions. 2.7 Step 7: Save the Project Template Download or copy the Unity project template to your computer. Save it in a location that is: Easy to find Separate from the original compressed file Included in your regular backup system For example: Documents/ └── Interactive Training Projects/ └── Training Project Template/

3 Create a Scene Step 1: Import the Videos into Unity Step 2: Create a New Scene Step 3: Add a Video to the Scene Step 4: Set up the Questions Step 5: Verify the Question Order Step 6: Edit the Beginning Screen Step 7: Edit the End Screen 3.1 Step 8: Add the Scene to the Build Profile 3.2 Step 9: Save the Scene 3.3 Scene Configuration Checklist

3 Create a Scene This chapter explains how to create and configure a scene using the Unity project template [Project Template Name]. In this project template, each scene corresponds to one video. For example, a training containing three videos will normally require three separate scenes. Step 1: Import the Videos into Unity Begin by importing the videos that you plan to use in the training. Locate the Project panel in Unity. Open the 2Project_Videos folder. Drag the video files from your computer into the Project_Videos folder. Wait for Unity to finish importing the files. Tip: Give each video a short, descriptive filename that reflects its position in the training sequence. For example: 01-introduction.mp4 02-demonstration.mp4 03-practice.mp4 Figure 3.1: Importing the project into Unity Hub. Step 2: Create a New Scene Create a new scene for each video in the training. In the Project panel, open the following path: Assets > 1_Scenes > Project_Scenes Important: Take into account that each scene represents one video, so if you have multiple scenes, you will have to create multiple scenes. In the right side you will see the following scenes 0_STARTING SCENE, 1_MyScene and LAST SCENE To create a new scene right click and change the name to 1_Scene_Name Use a sequence number and a descriptive title to keep the scenes organized. For example: 01_Introduction 02_Demonstration 03_Practice Write the instructions you want your participants to see at the beginning of the training. In the Hierarchy panel open the path: SCENE START > TRAINING_INTRODUCTION Step 3: Add a Video to the Scene After creating the scene, assign the corresponding video to it. Find the scene you want to upload the video on Assets > 1_Scenes > Project_Scenes > [YOUR SCENE NAME] Double-click the scene you want to upload the videos. Confirm that the correct scene name appears at the top of the Hierarchy panel. Important: It is not enough to click once on the scene. Unity has to open the object scene. Always make sure to double click. In the Hierarchy panel, open the following path: SceneStart > MENU > VIDEO PLAYER Select VIDEO PLAYER. Locate the Inspector panel. Find the Video Player subsection. Locate the Video Clip field. Drag the appropriate video from the 1_Project_Videos folder into the Video Clip field. Tip: Remember that you can find the videos in the Project panel in Assets > 1_Scenes > Project_Scenes Play the scene by double clicking on it to confirm that the correct video appears. Save the scene by pressing Ctrl + S. Step 4: Set up the Questions Note: In this section you will have to set up content of the instructions for each question, the content of each question, the content of the multiple answers for each questions, which answer will be considered correct, the content of the correct feedback (i.e., when answering correct) and corrective feedback (i.e., when answering incorrect) In the Project panel locate Assets > Prefabs Depending on the number of multiple answers you want Drag FourMultipleAnswers or TwoMultipleAnswers to the Hierarchy panel in the gray area Note: Make sure that it appears under SCENE START and not in one of is folders, as shown in the image. If it appears under SCENE START simply delete it and drag it again. Select FourMultipleAnswers or TwoMultipleAnswers and in the Inspector panel go the section EDIT EVERYTHING BELOW THIS: 3.1. Edit the content of the instruction text: change the text that says Write the content of the instructions here 3.2. Edit the content of the question: change the text that says Write the content of the question here 3.3. Edit the content of the multiple answers: write each answer right in the Answer Choice 1, … Each answer choice box corresponds to one of the answers. 3.4. Edit the content of the correct feedback: change the text that says Write your correct feedback here 3.5. Edit the content of the corrective feedback: change the text that says Write your corrective feedback here To choose which answer will be considered as correct, write the number of the answer in the Conrrect Answer Choice. Repeat steps 3 and 4 for each question you have. Note: You have the option of providing more detailes corrective feedback if the participant answers wrong multiple times the same question. You can write more detailed feedback on the subsections Corrective Feedback Text 2, Corrective Feedback Tex 3 and Corrective Feedback Tex 4. Use the Scene Manager to determine when questions will appear during the video. 3.0.1 Open the Dynamic Sequence Setup In the Hierarchy panel, select SCENCE MANAGER in the the following path: SCENE START > MENU > SCENE MANAGER Go to the Inspector panel, locate Dynamic Sequence Setup. You will see two main lists: List Purpose Question Canvases Determines which question appears at each pause. In other words, the order of questions Pause At Determines when the video pauses for each question om seconds 3.0.1.1 Configure Question Canvases Drag the questions objects that you have from the Hierarchy panel to the Question Canvases Write the time in the video where the questions should appear. Take into account that the numbers are in second, and they should correspond to the seconds in the Video. 3.0.1.2 Configure the Pause Times Set the number of items equal to the number of questions in the scene. Enter the video time at which each question should appear. Arrange the timestamps in chronological order. For example: Item Pause time 0 60 seconds 1 120 seconds 2 180 seconds In this example, the questions will appear at 1:00, 2:00, and 3:00 minutes. 3.0.2 Verify that the scenes are connected Open the scenes in the Project panel ASSETS > 1_SCENES > PROJECT SCENES Leave the scenes open In the Hierarchy panel, in the subsection Scence Settingswrite the name of the scene that will appear after the video ends. If it is the end of the tutorial, write LAST SCENE, so that it connects to the last scene of the project. Step 5: Verify the Question Order Before continuing, confirm that: Every pause time has a corresponding question canvas. The pause times are arranged chronologically. The question canvases are arranged in the intended order. The number of items in both lists is identical. Each question is associated with the correct point in the video. Warning: Questions may appear at the wrong times when the order of the question canvases does not match the order of the pause times. Step 6: Edit the Beginning Screen The beginning screen introduces the training before the video starts. Locate BeginSceneCanvas in the Hierarchy panel. Expand the canvas to locate its text elements. Select the header text. In the Inspector panel, replace the existing text with the introduction for your training. Save the scene. For example: Welcome to the caregiver training. In this module, you will observe a caregiver responding to a child during a daily routine. Keep the introduction: Brief Easy to read Relevant to the video Consistent with the purpose of the training module Step 7: Edit the End Screen The end screen determines which scene will open after the current scene is completed. Locate End Screen inside the SceneStart prefab. Select the field that identifies the next scene. Enter the exact name of the scene that should open next. For example: 02 Demonstration Important: The scene name must be written exactly as it appears in the Unity scene list. Differences in capitalization, spacing, or punctuation may prevent Unity from opening the scene. 3.0.3 Configure the Final Scene When the current scene is the final scene in the training, enter: Last Scene Make sure that Last Scene matches the text expected by the project template exactly. 3.1 Step 8: Add the Scene to the Build Profile Every new scene must be added to the Unity build profile. Open the scene that you want to add. Select File from the Unity menu. Select Build Profiles. Click Open Scene List. Click Add Open Scenes. Confirm that the scene appears in the scene list. Repeat this process whenever you create a new scene. Important: A scene that is not included in the build profile may work inside the Unity Editor but fail to open in the completed application. 3.2 Step 9: Save the Scene Save the scene after making any changes by pressing: Ctrl + S You should save the scene after: Assigning a video Changing question timestamps Adding question canvases Editing the beginning screen Editing the end screen Adding the scene to the build profile 3.3 Scene Configuration Checklist Before moving to the next scene, confirm that: The scene has a clear and unique name. The correct video has been assigned. The video plays correctly. The number of pause times matches the number of questions. Each question canvas is in the correct position. The beginning-screen text has been updated. The next-scene name has been entered correctly. The scene has been added to the build profile. The scene has been saved. After completing this checklist, repeat the process for the next video in the training.
